root_text.sn35.13:1.2: “Pubbeva me, bhikkhave, sambodhā anabhisambuddhassa bodhisattasseva sato etadahosi:
root_text.sn35.13:1.3: ‘ko nu kho cakkhussa assādo, ko ādīnavo, kiṁ nissaraṇaṁ?
root_text.sn35.13:1.8: ko manassa assādo, ko ādīnavo, kiṁ nissaraṇan’ti?
root_text.sn35.13:1.10: ‘yaṁ kho cakkhuṁ paṭicca uppajjati sukhaṁ somanassaṁ, ayaṁ cakkhussa assādo.
root_text.sn35.13:1.11: Yaṁ cakkhuṁ aniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vipariṇāmadhammaṁ, ayaṁ cakkhussa ādīnavo.
root_text.sn35.13:1.12: Yo cakkhusmiṁ chandarāgavinayo chandarāgappahānaṁ, idaṁ cakkhussa nissaraṇaṁ.
root_text.sn35.13:1.15: yaṁ jivhaṁ paṭicca uppajjati sukhaṁ somanassaṁ, ayaṁ jivhāya assādo.
root_text.sn35.13:1.16: Yaṁ jivhā aniccā dukkhā vipariṇāmadhammā, ayaṁ jivhāya ādīnavo.
root_text.sn35.13:1.17: Yo jivhāya chandarāgavinayo chandarāgappahānaṁ, idaṁ jivhāya nissaraṇaṁ.
root_text.sn35.13:1.19: yaṁ manaṁ paṭicca uppajjati sukhaṁ somanassaṁ, ayaṁ manassa assādo.
root_text.sn35.13:1.20: Yaṁ mano anicco dukkho vipariṇāmadhammo, ayaṁ manassa ādīnavo.
root_text.sn35.13:1.21: Yo manasmiṁ chandarāgavinayo chandarāgappahānaṁ, idaṁ manassa nissaraṇan’ti.
root_text.sn35.13:2.1: Yāvakīvañcāhaṁ, bhikkhave, imesaṁ channaṁ ajjhattikānaṁ āyatanānaṁ evaṁ assādañca assādato, ādīnavañca ādīnavato, nissaraṇañca nissaraṇato yathābhūtaṁ nābbhaññāsiṁ, neva tāvāhaṁ, bhikkhave, sadevake loke samārake sabrahmake sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya ‘anuttaraṁ sammāsambodhiṁ abhisambuddho’ti paccaññāsiṁ.
root_text.sn35.13:2.2: Yato ca khvāhaṁ, bhikkhave, imesaṁ channaṁ ajjhattikānaṁ āyatanānaṁ evaṁ assādañca assādato, ādīnavañca ādīnavato, nissaraṇañca nissaraṇato yathābhūtaṁ abbhaññāsiṁ, athāhaṁ, bhikkhave, sadevake loke samārake sabrahmake sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya ‘anuttaraṁ sammāsambodhiṁ abhisambuddho’ti paccaññāsiṁ.
root_text.sn35.13:2.4: ‘akuppā me vimutti, ayamantimā jāti, natthi dāni punabbhavo’”ti.

translation_text.sn35.13:1.2: “Mendicants, before my awakening—when I was still unawakened but intent on awakening—I thought:
translation_text.sn35.13:1.3: ‘What’s the gratification, the drawback, and the escape when it comes to the eye …
translation_text.sn35.13:1.10: ‘The pleasure and happiness that arise from the eye: this is its gratification.
translation_text.sn35.13:1.11: That the eye is impermanent, suffering, and perishable: this is its drawback.
translation_text.sn35.13:1.12: Removing and giving up desire and greed for the eye: this is its escape.
translation_text.sn35.13:1.13: The pleasure and happiness that arise from the ear …
translation_text.sn35.13:1.20: That the mind is impermanent, suffering, and perishable: this is its drawback.
translation_text.sn35.13:1.21: Removing and giving up desire and greed for the mind: this is its escape.’
translation_text.sn35.13:2.1: As long as I didn’t truly understand these six interior sense fields’ gratification, drawback, and escape in this way for what they are, I didn’t announce my supreme perfect awakening in this world with its gods, Māras, and Divinities, this population with its ascetics and brahmins, its gods and humans.
translation_text.sn35.13:2.2: But when I did truly understand these six interior sense fields’ gratification, drawback, and escape in this way for what they are, I announced my supreme perfect awakening in this world with its gods, Māras, and Divinities, this population with its ascetics and brahmins, its gods and humans.
translation_text.sn35.13:2.3: Knowledge and vision arose in me:
translation_text.sn35.13:2.4: ‘My freedom is unshakable; this is my last rebirth; now there’ll be no more future lives.’”

reference_text.sn35.13:1.1: bj16.14, cck18.8, csp1ed13.241, csp2ed13.241, dr18.7, ms13S4_30, msdiv13, ndp14.8, pts-vp-pli4.7, sya18.8, vri26.7
